expectations.text: Uses 'Switch Statements' smell name or equivalent
expectations.evidence: Does not use 'Switch Statements' smell name. Labels the issue as 'Open/Closed Principle Violation' instead. No reference to the Refactoring Guru smell taxonomy.

expectations.text: Identifies Long Method smell by name
expectations.evidence: Mentions SRP violation and breaking into classes, but never names 'Long Method' smell specifically.
'SINGLE RESPONSIBILITY PRINCIPLE VIOLATION (Design)' — clearly identifies the method handles 5 distinct concerns.

expectations.text: Recommends at least one named refactoring technique
expectations.evidence: Mentions 'extract into separate methods or classes' but does not use canonical Refactoring Guru technique names like 'Extract Method' or 'Extract Class'.
Uses general software engineering terms (SRP, OCP, context managers, error handling) but not Refactoring Guru canonical smell/technique names.

expectations.text: Mentions Strategy pattern for the discount logic
expectations.evidence: 'Fix: Replace the chain with a dispatch dictionary or a strategy pattern.' — mentions Strategy in the OCP section.
